title: polypodium cambricum subsp. azoricum
intro: Polypodium cambricum subsp. azoricum, also known as Azores polypody, is a subspecies of the Polypodium cambricum species, a type of fern native to the Azores Islands in Portugal. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Polypodium cambricum subsp. azoricum include reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and acting as an antioxidant. The plant can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or decoction, and is also used in some folk remedies, although its effectiveness and safety should be evaluated by a healthcare professional before use.
